NetType: IANA Special Use
OriginAS: 
Organization: Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA)
RegDate: 1994-03-15
Updated: 2013-08-30
Comment: These addresses are in use by many millions of independently operated networks, which might be as small as a single computer connected to a home gateway, and are automatically configured in hundreds of millions of devices. They are only intended for use within a private context and traffic that needs to cross the Internet will need to use a different, unique address.
Comment: 
Comment: These addresses can be used by anyone without any need to coordinate with IANA or an Internet registry. The traffic from these addresses does not come from ICANN or IANA. We are not the source of activity you may see on logs or in e-mail records. Please refer to http://www.iana.org/abuse/answers
Comment: 
Comment: These addresses were assigned by the IETF, the organization that develops Internet protocols, in the Best Current Practice document, RFC 1918 which can be found at:
Comment: http://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc1918

attributes.magic: ASCII text, with CRLF line terminators
PE32 executable (GUI) Intel 80386 Mono/.Net assembly, for MS Windows

signature_info.copyright: Copyright © 2018 First National of Nebraska Inc.

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert icmp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any ( msg:"PROTOCOL-ICMP Unusual PING detected"; icode:0; itype:8; fragbits:!M; content:!"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWABCDEFGHI",depth 32; content:!"0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv",depth 32; content:!"EEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEE",depth 36; content:!"WANG2"; content:!"cacti-monitoring-system",depth 65; content:!"SolarWinds",depth 72; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop,ruleset community; reference:url,krebsonsecurity.com/2014/01/a-closer-look-at-the-target-malware-part-ii/; reference:url,krebsonsecurity.com/2014/01/a-first-look-at-the-target-intrusion-malware/; classtype:successful-recon-limited; sid:29456; rev:3; )

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET INFO Observed DNS Query to DynDNS Domain (linkpc .net)
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert dns $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"ET INFO Observed DNS Query to DynDNS Domain (linkpc .net)"; dns.query; content:".linkpc.net"; nocase; endswith; content:!"www.linkpc.net"; reference:url,dnsexit.com/domains/free-second-level-domains/; classtype:bad-unknown; sid:2034458; rev:3; metadata:affected_product Any, attack_target Client_Endpoint, created_at 2021_11_15, deployment Perimeter, performance_impact Low, signature_severity Informational, updated_at 2022_06_08;)
alert icmp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET any ( msg:"PROTOCOL-ICMP Echo Reply"; icode:0; itype:0; metadata:ruleset community; classtype:misc-activity; sid:408; rev:8; )

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: ADS Zone.Identifier Deleted By Uncommon Application
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the deletion of the "Zone.Identifier" ADS by an uncommon process. Attackers can leverage this in order to bypass security restrictions that make use of the ADS such as Microsoft Office apps.
Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, frack113 (split)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the use of the redirection character ">" to redicrect information in commandline

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert ( gid:122; sid:7; rev:2; msg:"(port_scan) TCP filtered portsweep"; metadata: rule-type preproc; classtype:attempted-recon;)
alert ( gid:116; sid:58; rev:2; msg:"(tcp) experimental TCP options found"; metadata: policy max-detect-ips drop, rule-type decode; classtype:protocol-command-decode;)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Chromium Browser Instance Executed With Custom Extension
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects a Chromium based browser process with the 'load-extension' flag to start a instance with a custom extension

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Potential Defense Evasion Via Binary Rename
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the execution of a renamed binary often used by attackers or malware leveraging new Sysmon OriginalFileName datapoint.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Matthew Green @mgreen27, Ecco, James Pemberton @4A616D6573, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The extension 'Subresource Filtering Rules' is a declarative Net Request rule set providing subresource filtering for Chrome. Manifest v2 only defines a name, version, and ruleset_format. No permissions, background or content scripts are declared, indicating no network access, data collection, or active code execution. This aligns with its advertised purpose of filtering unwanted resources.

attributes.magic: ASCII text, with no line terminators

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: APP-DETECT Apple Messages push.apple.com DNS TXT request attempt
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert udp $HOME_NET any -> any 53 ( msg:"APP-DETECT Apple Messages push.apple.com DNS TXT request attempt"; flow:to_server; byte_test:1,!&,0xF8,2; content:"|04|push|05|apple|03|com|00 00 10 00 01|",fast_pattern,nocase; service:dns; reference:url,www.apple.com/osx/apps/all.html#messages; classtype:policy-violation; gid:1; sid:25080; rev:3; )

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: (stream_tcp) data sent on stream after TCP reset sent
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert ( gid:129; sid:8; rev:2; msg:"(stream_tcp) data sent on stream after TCP reset sent"; metadata: policy max-detect-ips drop, rule-type preproc; classtype:protocol-command-decode;)

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
The code snippet appears to be a script written in Python. It begins by importing the 'os' module, which provides various functions for interacting with the operating system.

The main functionality of the script is to add a new crontab entry. A crontab is a file that specifies scheduled jobs (commands or scripts) to be run periodically by the cron daemon.

The code constructs a command using the 'cmd' variable. This command combines the output of two commands:

1. 'crontab -l': This command lists the existing crontab entries.
2. 'echo "@reboot sh /Users/{1}/{2}/startup.sh"': This command creates a new crontab entry that will execute the script 'startup.sh' located at '/Users/{1}/{2}' whenever the system reboots.

The output of the 'cmd' command is then piped through the 'grep' command to filter out any lines containing the phrase "no crontab". This ensures that the new crontab entry is only added if there is already an existing crontab.

Finally, the 'sort' command is used to sort the crontab entries, and the 'uniq' command is used to remove any duplicate entries. The resulting sorted and unique list of crontab entries is then passed to the 'crontab -' command, which updates the system's crontab with the new entries.

The 'os.system(cmd)' line executes the constructed command in the system shell, effectively adding the new crontab entry.

attributes.magic: Python script, ASCII text executable, with no line terminators

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: APP-DETECT Apple Messages push.apple.com DNS TXT request attempt
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert udp $HOME_NET any -> any 53 ( msg:"APP-DETECT Apple Messages push.apple.com DNS TXT request attempt"; flow:to_server; byte_test:1,!&,0xF8,2; content:"|04|push|05|apple|03|com|00 00 10 00 01|",fast_pattern,nocase; service:dns; reference:url,www.apple.com/osx/apps/all.html#messages; classtype:policy-violation; gid:1; sid:25080; rev:3; )

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
The code snippet appears to be a script written in Python. It begins by importing the 'os' module, which provides various functions for interacting with the operating system.

The main functionality of the script is to add a command to the crontab, which is a system utility used to schedule tasks to be executed periodically. The command that is added to the crontab is:

'(crontab -l ; echo "@reboot sh /home/{1}/.wine/drive_c/users/{1}/Application\ Data/{2}/startup.sh") 2>&1 | grep -v "no crontab" | sort | uniq | crontab -'

This command appears to be a complex pipeline of multiple commands. Let's break it down step by step:

1. 'crontab -l': This command lists the existing entries in the crontab.
2. 'echo "@reboot sh /home/{1}/.wine/drive_c/users/{1}/Application\ Data/{2}/startup.sh"': This command creates a new crontab entry that will execute the shell script located at '/home/{1}/.wine/drive_c/users/{1}/Application Data/{2}/startup.sh' at system reboot.
3. '2>&1': This redirects the standard error output of the previous command to the standard output.
4. 'grep -v "no crontab"': This command filters out any lines containing the string "no crontab" from the output of the previous command.
5. 'sort': This command sorts the output of the previous command.
6. 'uniq': This command removes any duplicate lines from the output of the previous command.
7. 'crontab -': This command finally adds the modified crontab entries to the system's crontab.

In summary, the script adds a crontab entry that will execute a shell script located at '/home/{1}/.wine/drive_c/users/{1}/Application Data/{2}/startup.sh' at system reboot. The exact behavior of the shell script is not evident from the provided code snippet.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: ADS Zone.Identifier Deleted By Uncommon Application
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the deletion of the "Zone.Identifier" ADS by an uncommon process. Attackers can leverage this in order to bypass security restrictions that make use of the ADS such as Microsoft Office apps.
Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, frack113 (split)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the use of the redirection character ">" to redicrect information in commandline

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert icmp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any ( msg:"PROTOCOL-ICMP Unusual PING detected"; icode:0; itype:8; fragbits:!M; content:!"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWABCDEFGHI",depth 32; content:!"0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv",depth 32; content:!"EEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEE",depth 36; content:!"WANG2"; content:!"cacti-monitoring-system",depth 65; content:!"SolarWinds",depth 72; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop,ruleset community; reference:url,krebsonsecurity.com/2014/01/a-closer-look-at-the-target-malware-part-ii/; reference:url,krebsonsecurity.com/2014/01/a-first-look-at-the-target-intrusion-malware/; classtype:successful-recon-limited; sid:29456; rev:3; )
